Alrighty, it's Andre here. I'm going to walk through the basic automation flow for an SPP stack. So you've got the screenshot inside of the lesson, but I'm just going to scroll down and just kind of unpack each component. And again, this is inside of ConvertKit. Most, if not all other ESPs will have a version of this. That will work in a very, very similar way. So translating this or porting across should be fairly, fairly simple. So at the top we have the trigger. This is a tag. So when a tag, when the SPP, in this case, in this example, when the auto-email SPP stack tag is added, it triggers this automation. So the first thing that happens is we remove the tag. So we do this because a tag can't be added if it's already. So if somebody already has a tag, it can't be re-added again. And therefore the automation can never be triggered if somebody already has this tag. So we remove the tag straight away so we can reuse it again in the future. So that's the first thing. So we add it, we take it away. The next thing we ask, we have a decision here. Does the purchased AoE tag exist? So this here is just, if somebody has this tag, it means that they're a customer. So in this case, are there a customer? If yes, end the automation. We don't want to send them through an SPP stack if they already own the course. So the next thing we have over here is we have our first custom field. So as you can see in the lesson, each SPP stack has its own custom field. So this example, it's SPP underscore AoE. All in lowercase, although convert could support uppercase. That's not always the case. So a rule of thumb is just keep custom fields as lowercase. So this one is, is this blank? Now any person who has never been 3A before, it's always going to be blank. So custom field always starts off blank unless you've written some string inside of there. So if it is blank, which is yes, go down this path here. If it is not blank, which means they've already received this. That's the only way it'll not be blank. If so, it's no, and then the automation ends in this example. The next example, we'll show you a variation of this. So when we go down here, SPP AoE 1. So this is subscribe this person to the sequence. And this sequence is the SPP 1 sequence. In this case, there's only one. So it subscribes them to that. The five minute wait over here. When testing this thing, at least in a kind of a kit, the minimum we can do is five minutes. So whenever I build these things, I always set this to five minutes. So then I can run through this myself and test it. But in the real world, this is, this is not going to be five minutes. It'll be longer than this. And again, what you choose to put over here is completely up to you. So what this means is, is wait five minutes before marking this, the sequence as complete. So if we don't want somebody to be able to re click the link, then this and then have this tag added that then puts them through this thing again. We could set that to say 24 hours, for example, 24 hours. You can set it for one month. If you didn't want somebody to by mistake go through this thing again. But I think that's, that's probably too long. So let's just say it's 24 hours. So once you've tested this thing, you will then increase this to 24 hours or whatever makes sense to you. These two over here. So if while this sequence is running, this could be anything from four to nine emails or that's our recommendation. If they're going through that sequence and they make a purchase, we want to remove them from the, from the SPP. So that's what this thing does is if the purchase a we tag is added, which means they made a purchase, pull them forward. So it removes them from the sequence. It doesn't matter which email they on, it'll pull them forward and it'll complete the sequence and end the automation at which point they'll never be able to go through here again because they will have the purchase tag. For everybody else. So if somebody runs through here, they, they go through the pause. They're still not a customer at that point. It adds this tag, which then triggers this side of the, of the flow, which then pulls them forward and ends, ends the automation essentially. So it'll then mark, it'll mark the, the, this thing is complete. So the complete tag is, is added, which then satisfies this, this condition here, which then pulls them forward and then we remove the tag. So it can be added again in the future. Now over here is the final part of the custom field. So inside of here, we can see it writes this field to the custom field. SPP, AOE one completed. You can have whatever you want inside you. That, that makes sense for you. You'll see in, in the subsequent videos that we then use this, we, we then check to see if somebody has this. And if it does, we know that they've, they've been down the first part of this. So that's this. It's simple. You're never going to use it like this as we've explained in the lesson. So the next video, I'll show the enhancement tweak to this. Okay.
